events.description: Chief of Branch of Realty H. M. Critchfield issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee, enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 office letter
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to superintendents
Billings Area Office transmitted subsequent fee patent policy statement to superintendents
Billings Area Office transmitted copy of November 22, 1955 office memorandum re fee patent policy to Superintendent, Northern Cheyenne
Central Office issued memorandum on fee patent policy referenced in November 7, 1955 transmittal
Central Office issued memorandum on disposition of revenue from submarginal lands, quoted in November 29, 1955 Billings Area memorandum
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to Superintendents regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
Billings Area Office issued memorandum transmitting committee reports from Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955, with committee appointments and reports on land operations, conservation, and reporting systems
Committee appointed at Land Operations Conference on June 14, 1955, chaired by K. S. Engel of Turtle Mountain
Committee made preliminary report to the conference on June 14, 1955
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 contained amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of grazing fees from submarginal lands

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory survey conducted by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock, transferred to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Program planned to step up soil survey on Pine Ridge and Rosebud to keep ahead of conservationists' needs
Process of completing formal soil survey work plan with SCS for Shannon County on Pine Ridge

financial_transactions.description: Grazing fees collected for submarginal land grazing privileges held in special deposit pending legislation to transfer lands and fees to respective tribes
Funds held in special deposits for submarginal land grazing fees to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified

Accrued construction, operation and maintenance charges required to be paid or contracted for under 25 CFR 151 and Act of March 7, 1928; delinquent O&M charges must be deducted from land sale proceeds per Bid Form 5-114

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee as equitable investment for lessees on Indian lands

relationships.subject: Aberdeen Area
relationships.object: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule, Winnebago, Cheyenne River, Standing Rock Reservations
relationships.context: BIA Aberdeen Area comprises approximately 6.5 million acres on various reservations in North Dakota, South Dakota, and northeastern Nebraska

relationships.subject: Act of March 7, 1928
relationships.context: Creates first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs

relationships.subject: Indian women married to white men
relationships.context: Committee recommended declaring Indian women married to white men ineligible for grazing allocations as non-Indian husbands effectively receive the subsidy

relationships.subject: lessees
relationships.context: Lessees recommended to be given preference to meet high bids and renew permits for better range management

relationships.subject: Rex Carey
relationships.context: Previously served as Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock before transfer to Pine Ridge

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in all patent requests to allow expeditious handling
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum distributed; Billings Area Office forwarded to superintendents January 6, 1955

correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Realty staff to obtain information on rights of way and include in transmittals with patent applications or when Indian lands advertised for public sale are traversed by approved rights of way
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum issued enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 office letter

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants - clarification of policy following May 16 memorandum
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Policy statement distributed; temporary manual release to be issued; Billings Area forwarded to Northern Cheyenne Superintendent on November 7, 1955

correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy as set forth in memorandum of May 10 and subsequent policy statements of June 3 and October 4, 1955
correspondence.outcome: Copy of November 22, 1955 office memorandum attached and transmitted

correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Any funds held in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury before end of calendar year; future revenues to be deposited to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum issued quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Comments requested on conclusions reached by several committees
correspondence.outcome: Committee reports from June 13-15, 1955 Sioux City conference attached for information

correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Each Superintendent to establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment requirements before land sale transactions are finally completed
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum issued; Bureau auditors had called attention to failures to obtain payment for accrued charges

correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands - amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
correspondence.action_requested: Funds in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719; future revenues to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Quoted in Billings Area memorandum of November 29, 1955

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted, creating first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects where construction, operation and maintenance costs remain unpaid, directing lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands
Amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 published in Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777, deleting reference to deposit of submarginal land grazing fees in special deposits
Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; funds previously held in special deposit in support of this legislation
